briefdescription.para: Structure to hold simulation parameters parsed by the parameter file.
Global host data that holds the metric components for the simulation grid $ g_{μν}, g^{μν},\ \rm{and}\ \sqrt{- g} $ .
Global host reusable workspace for tracking sub-intervals and error estimates during adaptive numerical integration used by GSL.

detaileddescription.para: Global host data array of precomputed emissivity values binned by frequency.
Global host data array containing precomputed superphotons weights binned by frequency.
Global host data counter to keep track of the number of scattered superphotons per scattering layer.

briefdescription.para: Global host data that holds the number of superphotons recorded into the spectrum.

detaileddescription.para: Global host variable representing the physical inner boundary of the simulation grid, e.g, the starting point of the physical domain.
Global host variable representing the physical outer boundary of the simulation grid. It defines the edge of the grid for the simulation.
Global host variable representing the event horizon of the black hole in Kerr-Schild coordinates
Global host variable representing a shift in the logarithmic radial coordinate.
Global host variable for the latitude slope for Modified Kerr-Schild (MKS) coordinates
Global host array defining the start coordinate of the simulation grid in each dimension in native coordinates.
Global host array defining the stop coordinate of the simulation grid in each dimension in native coordinates.
Global host array defining the grid spacing in each dimension in native coordinates.
Global host variable for the gas adiabatic index $ \Gamma $.
Global host variable for the electron temperature unit to translate from cgs to code units.
Global host variable for the maximum optical depth found so far, used generally to calculate the biasing factor.
Global host variable calculating the total amount of mass falling into the black hole per unit time.
Global host variable for the advection luminosity in CGS units.
Global host variable for the bias normalization factor.
Global host pointer for accessing plasma properties.
Global host variable for the number of grid cells in the radial direction.
Global host variable for the number of grid cells in the poloidal direction.
Global host variable for the number of grid cells in the azimuthal direction.
Global host variable for the length unit conversion factor. Scales code units to centimeters (cm).
Global host variable for the magnetic field unit conversion factor. Scales code units to Gauss (G).
Global host variable for the electron number density unit conversion factor. Scales code units to $ \rm{cm}^-3$.
Global host variable for the mass density unit conversion factor. Scales code units to $\rm{g/cm}^3$.
Global host variable for the internal energy density unit conversion factor. Scales code units to $\rm{erg/cm}^3$.
Global host variable for the mass unit conversion factor. Scales code units to grams (g).
Global host variable for the time unit conversion factor. Scales code units to seconds (s).
Global host array for the precomputed hot cross section lookup table. It stores values indexed by photon energy ( $\rm{N}_{\omega}$) and electron temperature ( $\rm{N}_\rm{T}$).
Global host variable for the logarithmic step size in photon frequency ( $\Delta \ln \omega$). It determines the resolution of the frequency axis in hotcross section precomputed lookup table.

Global host variable for the logarithmic step size in electron temperature ( $\Delta \ln \Theta_\rm{e}$). It determines the resolution of the temperature axis in hotcross section precomputed lookup table.
Global host variable for the minimum logarithmic photon frequency ( $\ln \omega_{min}$). Defines the lower boundary of the frequency grid for cross-section calculations.
Global host variable for the minimum logarithmic electron temperature ( $\ln \Theta_{e,min}$). Defines the lower boundary of the temperature grid for cross-section calculations.
Global host array for the superphoton density distribution function.
Global host array for the precomputed values of the modified Bessel function of the second kind ( $K_2$).
Global host array for the maximum values of the differential photon number distribution ( $\frac{dN}{d\ln \nu}$). Used for normalization and sampling in the scattering process.
Global device array of curandState structures for random number generation on the GPU. It's sized to accommodate all threads across all blocks.

Global device array for the precomputed hot cross section lookup table. It stores values indexed by photon energy ( $\rm{N}_{\omega}$) and electron temperature ( $\rm{N}_\rm{T}$). It's the same as the host array table but accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable counter to keep track of the total number of superphotons generated from the grid during the simulation.
Global device variable to keep track of the number of superphotons recorded into the spectrum.
Global device variable for the target input number of superphotons to be generated in the simulation. It's the same as the host variable Ns but accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable counter to keep track of the total number of scatterings that have occurred during the simulation.
Global device variable for the electron temperature unit to translate from cgs to code units. It's the same as the host variable Thetae_unit but accessible on the GPU.
Global device array defining the start coordinate of the simulation grid in each dimension in native coordinates. It's the same as the host array startx but accessible on the GPU.
Global device array defining the grid spacing in each dimension in native coordinates. It's the same as the host array dx but accessible on the GPU.
Global device array containing precomputed superphotons weights binned by frequency. It's the same as the host array wgt but accessible on the GPU.
Global device array of precomputed emissivity values binned by frequency. It's the same as the host array F but accessible on the GPU.
Global device array for the precomputed modified Bessel function values binned by frequency. It's the same as the host array K2 but accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable for the bias normalization factor. It's the same as the host variable bias_norm but accessible on the GPU.
Global device array defining the stop coordinate of the simulation grid in each dimension in native coordinates. It's the same as the host array stopx but accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable representing the event horizon of the black hole in Kerr-Schild coordinates. It's the same as the host variable Rh but accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable for the maximum optical depth found so far, used generally to calculate the biasing factor. It's the same as the host variable max_tau_scatt but accessible on the GPU.

Global device variable counter to keep track of the number of scattered superphotons in the current scattering layer.
This is different than d_N_scatt which counts the total number of scattering events in the simulation.

Global device array to keep track of the number of scattered superphotons per scattering layer.
This is scattering_counter because scattered photons are stored layer by layer.
Global device variable counter to keep track of the number of photons being tracked in the current scattering batch. Used to dynamically allocate threads for photon tracking.
Global device array for the superphoton density distribution function. It mirrors the host array nint but is accessible on the GPU.
Global device array for the maximum values of the differential photon number distribution ( $\frac{dN}{d\ln \nu}$). It mirrors the host array dndlnu_max but is accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable for the latitude slope for Modified Kerr-Schild (MKS) coordinates It mirrors the host variable hslope but is accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable representing a shift in the logarithmic radial coordinate. It mirrors the host variable Rin but is accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable counter to keep track of the number of photons being sampled in the current tracking batch. Used to dynamically allocate threads for photon sampling.
Global device variable for the number of grid cells in the radial direction. It mirrors the host variable N1 but is accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable for the number of grid cells in the poloidal direction. It mirrors the host variable N2 but is accessible on the GPU.
Global device variable for the number of grid cells in the azimuthal direction. It mirrors the host variable N3 but is accessible on the GPU.

Global device variable for the ion-to-electron temperature ratio in the low-beta limit ( $R_{low}$). This defines the temperature ratio in highly magnetized regions (where $\beta \ll \beta_{\rm crit}$).

Global device variable for the ion-to-electron temperature ratio in the high-beta limit ( $R_{high}$). This defines the temperature ratio in weakly magnetized regions (where $\beta \gg \beta_{\rm crit}$).
Global device variable for the critical plasma beta value ( $\beta_{\rm crit}$) that delineates the transition between low-beta and high-beta temperature ratios.

Global device variable for the maximum allowed dimensionless electron temperature ( $\Theta_{e, max}$). This sets an upper limit on the electron temperature to prevent unphysical values during the simulation.
Global device variable for the ion-to-electron temperature ratio ( $T_p/T_e$).
Global device variable for the black hole mass in grams (g).
Global device variable for the length unit conversion factor. Scales code units to centimeters (cm).
Global device variable for the magnetic field unit conversion factor. Scales code units to Gauss (G).
Global device variable for the electron number density unit conversion factor. Scales code units to $ \rm{cm}^-3$.

briefdescription.para: Global host variable to hold simulation parameters parsed by the parameter file.
